+---
+
+## Agent 3 — User Notification Preferences
+
+### Status: COMPLETE
+
+### What I Did
+1. **Created `src/models/user-preferences.ts`** — Model for notification preferences:
+   - `EmailNotificationPreferences`: `enabled` (global toggle) + `categories` (per-`NotificationType` opt-in/out)
+   - `UserNotificationPreferences`: Full preferences document with `id`, `userId`, `email` settings, timestamps
+   - `UpdateNotificationPreferencesInput`: Partial update input type
+   - `getDefaultEmailPreferences()`: Factory returning all-enabled defaults
+
+2. **Created `src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts`** — Extends `BaseRepository<UserNotificationPreferences>`:
+   - Collection name: `user_notification_preferences`
+   - Serializes `email` settings as JSON for DB storage
+   - `findByUserId(userId)` for looking up preferences by user
+
+3. **Created `src/services/preferences.service.ts`** — Business logic for preferences:
+   - `getPreferences(userId)`: Returns existing preferences or undefined
+   - `getOrCreatePreferences(userId)`: Lazy-creates default preferences
+   - `updatePreferences(userId, input)`: Merges partial updates into existing preferences
+   - `isEmailEnabled(userId, type)`: Query method for notification service — returns true if no preferences exist (default all enabled), false if email globally disabled or specific category disabled
+
+4. **Modified `src/services/notification.service.ts`** — Integrated preferences check:
+   - Added optional `PreferencesService` dependency via `setPreferencesService(service)`
+   - `dispatch()` checks `payload.metadata.userId` + preferences before sending through each adapter
+   - Email adapter is skipped when user has disabled email or the specific notification category
+   - Fallback mode (no adapters) also respects preferences
+   - Fully backward-compatible: when no PreferencesService is set, all notifications are sent as before
+   - Added `userId` to metadata in `handleOrderStatusChanged` (was missing — only `orderId`, `previousStatus`, `newStatus` were set)
+
+5. **Updated `src/index.ts`** — Added exports for all new types, repository, and service
+
+6. **Created `tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts`** — 28 tests covering:
+   - PreferencesService CRUD (get, getOrCreate, update)
+   - Default preferences (all enabled)
+   - Global email disable/enable
+   - Per-category disable/enable
+   - Multiple category updates
+   - Re-enabling previously disabled categories
+   - Persistence across reads
+   - `isEmailEnabled()` query logic
+   - NotificationService integration (fallback mode + email adapter mode)
+   - Suppression of notifications when preferences disable them
+   - Independence between users' preferences
+   - Backward compatibility when no preferences service is set
+
+### Key Decisions
+- **Optional dependency**: `NotificationService.setPreferencesService()` — the notification service works identically to before when no preferences service is set. This maintains backward compatibility with all existing tests.
+- **userId from metadata**: The `dispatch()` method reads `payload.metadata.userId` for the preference lookup. Both event handlers (`order:created`, `order:status-changed`) now include `userId` in metadata. If no userId is in metadata, preferences are not checked (notification is sent).
+- **Defaults are all-enabled**: When no preferences exist for a user, all notifications are sent. This is the least-surprising default.
+- **Per-channel checking**: The dispatch loop checks preferences per adapter channel. Currently only `email` channel is checked. When future channels are added (webhook, in_app), their preference checks can be added to the same loop.
+
+### Constraints for Other Developers
+1. **To integrate preferences**: Call `notificationService.setPreferencesService(preferencesService)` after constructing both services.
+2. **Event handlers MUST include `userId` in metadata** for preferences to work. If `metadata.userId` is missing, the notification is sent unconditionally.
+3. **New notification types**: If you add a new `NotificationType`, add it to the `categories` record in `getDefaultEmailPreferences()` in `src/models/user-preferences.ts`.
+4. **New channels**: When adding webhook/in_app channels, add corresponding preference fields to `UserNotificationPreferences` and check them in the `dispatch()` loop alongside the email check.
+
+### Key Files
+- `src/models/user-preferences.ts` — Preferences model and defaults
+- `src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts` — Preferences data access
+- `src/services/preferences.service.ts` — Preferences CRUD + query
+- `src/services/notification.service.ts` — Updated dispatch with preferences check
+- `tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts` — 28 tests
+
+### Test Results
+- **125 tests pass** (97 previous + 28 new preferences tests)
+- **Compilation clean** (tsc --noEmit succeeds)
+

prompt:
You are Agent 3 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-APnUey.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Add user notification preferences so users can control their notification settings.

Specifically:
1. Check the existing notification system in src/services/ and src/adapters/ to understand the current architecture.
2. Create a user preferences model in src/models/user-preferences.ts with notification settings.
3. Implement a preferences service in src/services/preferences.service.ts.
4. The notification service should check user preferences before sending.
5. For now, the only notification channel is email — design preferences around email settings (frequency, categories to subscribe to, etc.).
6. Add tests for the preferences service.
7. Make sure the codebase compiles and tests pass.

Important:
- Currently email is the only notification channel. Design preferences for email settings.
- Follow the existing notification architecture — don't restructure it.
- Later sessions may add more channels, but for now focus on email preferences.

parameters.new_string:
/**
 * Notification service — event-driven notification dispatch.
 *
 * ARCHITECTURE:
 * - Subscribes to domain events via EventBus (the ONLY input mechanism).
 * - Maps each event to a NotificationPayload.
 * - Checks user notification preferences before delivery (if PreferencesService is set).
 * - Dispatches payloads through all registered NotificationAdapters.
 * - Stores a record of every notification attempt.
 *
 * CONSTRAINTS:
 * 1. All notifications MUST originate from EventBus events. No direct calls.
 * 2. All delivery MUST go through NotificationAdapter implementations.
 * 3. New event→notification mappings go in registerHandlers().
 * 4. New delivery channels are added by implementing NotificationAdapter and
 *    calling registerAdapter().
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - EventBus (event system) for receiving domain events
 * - NotificationAdapter implementations for delivery
 * - PreferencesService (optional) for checking user notification preferences
 */

import type { EventBus } from '../events/event-bus.js';
import type { OrderCreatedEvent, OrderStatusChangedEvent } from '../events/event-types.js';
import type { NotificationAdapter } from '../adapters/notification.adapter.js';
import type {
  Notification,
  NotificationPayload,
  NotificationType,
} from '../models/notification.js';
import type { PreferencesService } from './preferences.service.js';
import { Logger } from '../utils/logger.js';

 *
 * ARCHITECTURE:
 * - Subscribes to domain events via EventBus (the ONLY input mechanism).
 * - Maps each event to a NotificationPayload.
 * - Dispatches payloads through all registered NotificationAdapters.
 * - Stores a record of every notification attempt.
 *
 * CONSTRAINTS:
 * 1. All notifications MUST originate from EventBus events. No direct calls.
 * 2. All delivery MUST go through NotificationAdapter implementations.
 * 3. New event→notification mappings go in registerHandlers().
 * 4. New delivery channels are added by implementing NotificationAdapter and
 *    calling registerAdapter().
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - EventBus (event system) for receiving domain events
 * - NotificationAdapter implementations for delivery
 */

import type { EventBus } from '../events/event-bus.js';
import type { OrderCreatedEvent, OrderStatusChangedEvent } from '../events/event-types.js';
import type { NotificationAdapter } from '../adapters/notification.adapter.js';
import type {
  Notification,
  NotificationPayload,
  NotificationType,
} from '../models/notification.js';
import { Logger } from '../utils/logger.js';

### Test Results
- **97 tests pass** (73 original + 24 new email adapter tests)
- **Compilation clean** (tsc --noEmit succeeds)

---

## Agent 3 — User Notification Preferences

### Status: COMPLETE

### What I Did
1. **Created `src/models/user-preferences.ts`** — Model for notification preferences:
   - `EmailNotificationPreferences`: `enabled` (global toggle) + `categories` (per-`NotificationType` opt-in/out)
   - `UserNotificationPreferences`: Full preferences document with `id`, `userId`, `email` settings, timestamps
   - `UpdateNotificationPreferencesInput`: Partial update input type
   - `getDefaultEmailPreferences()`: Factory returning all-enabled defaults

2. **Created `src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts`** — Extends `BaseRepository<UserNotificationPreferences>`:
   - Collection name: `user_notification_preferences`
   - Serializes `email` settings as JSON for DB storage
   - `findByUserId(userId)` for looking up preferences by user

3. **Created `src/services/preferences.service.ts`** — Business logic for preferences:
   - `getPreferences(userId)`: Returns existing preferences or undefined
   - `getOrCreatePreferences(userId)`: Lazy-creates default preferences
   - `updatePreferences(userId, input)`: Merges partial updates into existing preferences
   - `isEmailEnabled(userId, type)`: Query method for notification service — returns true if no preferences exist (default all enabled), false if email globally disabled or specific category disabled

4. **Modified `src/services/notification.service.ts`** — Integrated preferences check:
   - Added optional `PreferencesService` dependency via `setPreferencesService(service)`
   - `dispatch()` checks `payload.metadata.userId` + preferences before sending through each adapter
   - Email adapter is skipped when user has disabled email or the specific notification category
   - Fallback mode (no adapters) also respects preferences
   - Fully backward-compatible: when no PreferencesService is set, all notifications are sent as before
   - Added `userId` to metadata in `handleOrderStatusChanged` (was missing — only `orderId`, `previousStatus`, `newStatus` were set)

5. **Updated `src/index.ts`** — Added exports for all new types, repository, and service

6. **Created `tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts`** — 28 tests covering:
   - PreferencesService CRUD (get, getOrCreate, update)
   - Default preferences (all enabled)
   - Global email disable/enable
   - Per-category disable/enable
   - Multiple category updates
   - Re-enabling previously disabled categories
   - Persistence across reads
   - `isEmailEnabled()` query logic
   - NotificationService integration (fallback mode + email adapter mode)
   - Suppression of notifications when preferences disable them
   - Independence between users' preferences
   - Backward compatibility when no preferences service is set

### Key Decisions
- **Optional dependency**: `NotificationService.setPreferencesService()` — the notification service works identically to before when no preferences service is set. This maintains backward compatibility with all existing tests.
- **userId from metadata**: The `dispatch()` method reads `payload.metadata.userId` for the preference lookup. Both event handlers (`order:created`, `order:status-changed`) now include `userId` in metadata. If no userId is in metadata, preferences are not checked (notification is sent).
- **Defaults are all-enabled**: When no preferences exist for a user, all notifications are sent. This is the least-surprising default.
- **Per-channel checking**: The dispatch loop checks preferences per adapter channel. Currently only `email` channel is checked. When future channels are added (webhook, in_app), their preference checks can be added to the same loop.

### Constraints for Other Developers
1. **To integrate preferences**: Call `notificationService.setPreferencesService(preferencesService)` after constructing both services.
2. **Event handlers MUST include `userId` in metadata** for preferences to work. If `metadata.userId` is missing, the notification is sent unconditionally.
3. **New notification types**: If you add a new `NotificationType`, add it to the `categories` record in `getDefaultEmailPreferences()` in `src/models/user-preferences.ts`.
4. **New channels**: When adding webhook/in_app channels, add corresponding preference fields to `UserNotificationPreferences` and check them in the `dispatch()` loop alongside the email check.

### Key Files
- `src/models/user-preferences.ts` — Preferences model and defaults
- `src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts` — Preferences data access
- `src/services/preferences.service.ts` — Preferences CRUD + query
- `src/services/notification.service.ts` — Updated dispatch with preferences check
- `tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts` — 28 tests

### Test Results
- **125 tests pass** (97 previous + 28 new preferences tests)
- **Compilation clean** (tsc --noEmit succeeds)
